ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'cola beverage' class; no Pepsi-brand-specific entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KL covers cola-type soft drinks as a category; no Pepsi-specific branded code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 refers to cola drink as a substance class; Pepsi is a branded cola. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 355 mL can of Pepsi Cola, equivalent to approximately 368 g, as commonly served in Singapore and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated beverage, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for carbonated cola beverage with caffeine matches Pepsi Cola composition. Values per 100 g used directly. Pepsi is a branded cola; USDA SR Legacy generic cola is the approved closest match under source policy.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, cola, Pepsi — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 2008)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pepsi Cola GI of 63 (medium) is directly cited in the University of Sydney GI Database and in Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 38.9) / 100 ≈ 24.5, based on 368 g serving containing 10.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g (38.9 g per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g); however, high sugar load may contribute to metabolic risk factors associated with hypertension
hypertension.guidance: Pepsi Cola is very low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertensive risk from salt, but its high free-sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g) may contribute to weight gain and insulin resistance, which are secondary drivers of elevated blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g, entirely as added sugars) and medium glycemic index of 63 producing a high glycemic load per standard serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pepsi Cola has a medium GI of 63 and delivers approximately 38.9 g of rapidly absorbed free sugars per standard 355 mL can, resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 24.5; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption as it causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation without providing satiety or nutritional benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar intake from regular consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and increased cardiovascular risk; zero fat and zero saturated fat are favorable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Pepsi Cola contains no fat or saturated fat, its high free-sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g) consumed regularly is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimize consumption in line with WHO free-sugar guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pepsi Cola is a globally recognized carbonated cola soft drink manufactured by PepsiCo, sold in cans, bottles, and fountain format worldwide including Singapore. It is an ultra-processed, ready-to-drink beverage produced by combining carbonated water with high-fructose corn syrup or sugar, caramel color, phosphoric acid, natural flavors, and caffeine. The beverage is characterized by a sweet, slightly acidic cola flavor with a crisp effervescence and dark brown color. Per 100 g, Pepsi Cola provides approximately 41 kcal, 10.6 g of carbohydrate (entirely as free sugars), 0 g protein, and 0 g fat. It contains negligible sodium (4 mg per 100 g) and trace minerals. Pepsi Cola has a glycemic index of 63 (medium) and delivers a high glycemic load of approximately 24.5 per standard 355 mL can due to its concentrated sugar content. It provides no dietary fiber, no vitamins, and no meaningful micronutrients. Clinically, Pepsi Cola is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid postprandial glucose elevation from free sugars, of moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to triglyceride-raising effects of regular high-sugar intake, and of low direct concern for hypertension due to negligible sodium. In Singapore, Pepsi Cola is widely available at hawker centres, food courts, restaurants, and convenience stores, often served chilled or over ice as an accompaniment to meals. It is sold under the Pepsi brand in Chinese as 百事可乐 (Bǎishì kělè), in Tamil as பெப்சி கோலா, and in Hindi as पेप्सी कोला.
common_user_queries: How much sugar in a can of Pepsi?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for carbonated cola beverage with caffeine (FDC ID 174851), which is an exact category match for Pepsi Cola. GI value of 63 sourced directly from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables, which include Pepsi Cola as a named entry. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — cola beverages contain no protein by formulation. Fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — cola beverages contain no fat by formulation. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero as no fat is present. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — cola beverages contain no dietary fiber. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — no animal-derived ingredients. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food. Cuisine tag in input was 'Malaysian' reflecting the field survey data restaurant context; region_of_origin corrected to United States as Pepsi is an American global brand. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. GI of 63 is a medium GI value; glycemic_load_per_serving of 24.5 calculated for a 368 g (355 mL) standard can serving.
